notes: The Department of Classics is committed to the study of ancient Greece and Rome and their political, social, artistic, and intellectual legacy. The area of inquiry spans more than two thousand years, from the art and archaeology of the Mediterranean Bronze Age (ca. 1700 B.C.E.) to the breakdown of the Roman empire in Late Antiquity (ca. 400 C.E.). The interdisciplinary nature of the program offers its students a broad range of courses in the fields of language, literature, religion, mythology, philosophy, political history, cultural studies, digital humanities, virtual reality, archaeology, art, and film. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete as many of the following by the end of spring before transfer: One course in Classical Mythology, One course in Archaeology and One course in Greek or Latin Literature in Translation. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://classics.ucla.edu/undergraduate/majors-and-minors/classical-civilization and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
